task_completion.description: Open or read the DRM recognition dataset.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A spreadsheet viewer, text editor, or terminal showing the contents or loading of ~/pebl/data/drm_recognition_data.csv.

task_completion.description: Identify and exclude the participant with corrupted data based on the specified criteria.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code, script, or data manipulation steps showing filtering logic for mean RT < 100ms and 100% 'Yes' responses, or explicitly identifying 'sub-99' for exclusion.

task_completion.description: Compute the True Recognition Rate, Critical False Memory Rate, and Baseline False Alarm Rate for each valid participant.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code or spreadsheet formulas calculating rates based on 'studied', 'critical_lure', and 'unrelated' conditions.

task_completion.description: Compute the average of the three metrics across all valid participants.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code or spreadsheet formulas calculating the mean of the previously computed individual rates.

task_completion.description: Structure the output as a JSON object with 'participants' and 'group_means' keys, including exclusion details.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A script or text editor showing the construction of a JSON structure with the required keys, individual metrics, and exclusion details (id, reason).

task_completion.description: Save the final JSON report to the specified output path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file explorer, terminal command, or editor showing the file being saved to exactly ~/pebl/analysis/drm_report.json.

integrity.description: The agent actually computed the metrics rather than hardcoding fake results.
integrity.visual_evidence: Presence of a script (e.g., Python, R) or spreadsheet formulas performing the calculations, rather than just typing a JSON file from scratch.

integrity.description: The agent used the specified file paths for input and output.
integrity.visual_evidence: The input file path ~/pebl/data/drm_recognition_data.csv and output file path ~/pebl/analysis/drm_report.json are visible in the commands or scripts.

integrity.description: The agent did not invent participant IDs or data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The participant IDs in the output JSON match those seen in the input dataset or script logic (e.g., 'sub-99' is explicitly handled as the excluded participant).

privileged_info_for_vlm: The contaminated participant that must be excluded is 'sub-99', who has 100% 'yes' responses and reaction times between 20ms and 80ms. The final output must be saved exactly to '/home/ga/pebl/analysis/drm_report.json'.
